commands.author: mushuiyu886

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause provider catalog and routing fix for the bundled `opencode` Zen provider.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High — the current PR head has fresh authenticated provider proof, family-specific live adapter proof, and focused regression coverage.
- Restore a plugin-owned OpenCode Zen model catalog for the bundled `opencode` provider.
- Add static rows for the 48 models currently advertised by Zen, plus runtime live discovery for `/zen/v1/models` when credentials are available.
- Restore dynamic model resolution, catalog augmentation, family-specific transport routing, and manifest metadata for manifest-first flows.

Fixes #92479

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `opencode` Zen provider was migrated as an auth/setup surface without owning the Zen model catalog contract that `opencode-go` already supported. As a result, the provider registry had no source-of-truth model rows, no dynamic resolver for `opencode/<model>` references, and no family-aware transport mapping for Zen model IDs.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch restores the missing provider-owned catalog/resolver boundary instead of adding downstream string special cases. Runtime catalog loading now resolves real Zen rows when discovery succeeds, keeps a local seed available when discovery is unavailable, and maps each model family at the provider boundary before the generic LLM adapters run.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users who configure OpenCode Zen can select advertised Zen models directly instead of hand-registering every model or seeing `opencode/<model>` fail as unknown/unavailable.
- What did NOT change: This does not change non-OpenCode providers, does not change `opencode-go` runtime behavior, and does not expand the generic LLM adapter APIs outside this provider's catalog/routing contract.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The provider plugin is the source-of-truth owner for bundled provider catalog metadata. `check-upstream-fixed` found main does not already cover this PR, and the remaining product decision is seed scope/ownership, not a competing implementation.
- Patch quality notes: The patch-quality warnings are expected for this provider-catalog repair: the static seed is a deliberate offline guard, default/undefined returns are catalog parsing boundaries, and the larger manifest diff is generated catalog metadata backed by live provider proof rather than unrelated refactor.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: The bundled `opencode` Zen provider exposed auth/setup but did not expose a model catalog or dynamic model resolver, so `opencode/<model>` selections failed as unknown or unavailable. This patch restores the provider-owned Zen catalog, keeps a 48-row seed visible when live discovery is unavailable, resolves shared `opencode-go` credentials for the Zen catalog, and routes representative Zen model families through the expected OpenClaw adapters.
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `e5497442`, authenticated OpenCode Zen key present but redacted. This machine requires `NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1` plus uppercase proxy variables for Node 22 outbound fetch/SDK traffic.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
source "$EVIDENCE_DIR/context.env"
OPENCODE_API_KEY=<redacted> \
  HTTPS_PROXY="$https_proxy" HTTP_PROXY="$http_proxy" ALL_PROXY="$all_proxy" NO_PROXY="$no_proxy" \
  NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1 \
  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec tsx \
  "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-zen-live-proof.mts" > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-zen-live-proof.log" 2>&1

corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec vitest run extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix: Authenticated discovery returned the 48-row Zen catalog with an Authorization header present, the provider catalog resolved using shared `opencode-go` credentials, and representative live completions returned `ok` through the affected OpenClaw adapter families.

```text
OpenCode Zen live proof at 2026-06-13T03:27:09.724Z
worktree=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-92495
credential=present (redacted)
node_env_proxy=enabled
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=claude-haiku-4-5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=GPT model=gpt-5-nano api=openai-responses baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gpt-5-nano stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gemini-3-flash stream=undefined max_tokens=undefined stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=qwen3.6-plus stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request_attempt family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.7 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 result=failed error="unexpected text: <empty>; content="
live_request family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.5 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=minimax-m2.5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
result=passed
```

- Observed result after fix: `opencode` now exposes a 48-model Zen catalog, can populate the catalog from live discovery with a redacted real credential, accepts the shared OpenCode credential path from `opencode-go`, and successfully completes real provider calls through Claude/Anthropic messages, GPT/OpenAI responses, Gemini/Google generative AI, Qwen/Anthropic messages, and MiniMax/OpenAI chat completions routes.
- What was not tested: I did not live-call all 48 Zen models individually. The authenticated discovery proof used the provider-catalog live helper with a direct fetch guard because this local machine's default SSRF-guarded fetch path times out behind the configured proxy; the production no-discovery path remains covered by static catalog tests, and the real model requests above exercised the actual OpenClaw runtime provider adapters. `minimax-m2.7` returned an empty stream body in this account/environment, so the MiniMax family proof used `minimax-m2.5`, which returned `ok` through the same `openai-completions` route.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus live evidence script `opencode-zen-live-proof.mts` stored outside the repo evidence directory.
- Scenario locked in: Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, and Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: A focused provider test locks the source-of-truth catalog/routing contract without adding broad adapter mocks, while the live proof covers the external provider behavior that unit tests cannot prove.

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-92495

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)
Duration  44.99s
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — authenticated discovery and representative live requests:** Addressed by the redacted authenticated `/zen/v1/models` request (`authorization_header=present status=200`) and live `ok` completions through Claude, GPT, Gemini, Qwen, and MiniMax adapter families.
- **RF-002 — stronger real behavior proof:** Addressed by the authenticated external Zen proof above; this is no longer limited to public discovery, static catalog listing, or unit tests.
- **RF-003 — shared credentials and affected adapters:** Addressed by `shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48` plus live requests through `anthropic-messages`, `openai-responses`, `google-generative-ai`, and `openai-completions`.
- **RF-004 — static catalog ownership/synchronization risk:** The 48-row seed is intentionally provider-owned so the Zen provider remains usable when live discovery is unavailable. Live discovery is still preferred whenever credentials are present; if the provider owner wants a smaller seed or different ownership policy, that is a product/maintenance decision rather than a code defect in this patch.
- **RF-005 — routing defect and maintainer decision boundary:** The routing defect is covered by tests and live requests. The remaining maintainer decision is the acceptable seed scope for Zen, not whether the restored catalog/routing behavior works.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, provider catalog/routing, generated manifest metadata, and large-diff catalog seed review cost.
- Risk explanation: The compatibility risk is that a provider-owned seed can drift from Zen if the upstream catalog changes before live discovery runs. The auth-provider risk is bounded to resolving the same OpenCode credential across `opencode` and `opencode-go`; no key is stored in the repo or PR body, and live proof used a redacted environment variable only. The large diff is mostly generated manifest/catalog rows rather than a cross-cutting runtime refactor.
- Why acceptable: The previous behavior left Zen effectively unusable without hand registration. The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, preserves a safe no-credential/no-discovery local seed, and now has real provider proof across the affected adapter families.
